models.generation_context: JL-gen Wrangler refreshed for 2024 MY; 2026 MY is a carryover year with new quick-release door hinges, new exterior colors (Reign, Goldilocks, Joose, Earl Grey, Tuscadero) and a new 85th Anniversary limited edition. The discontinued 2025 Rubicon 392 (V8) is replaced by the new Moab 392 trim, split as its own model entry per the performance-variant rule.
models.model_summary: Body-on-frame mid-size off-road SUV available in 2-door and 4-door body styles with removable doors and roof. Six trim grades (Sport / Sport S / Willys / Sahara / Rubicon / Rubicon X) cover capability levels from basic 4WD to factory beadlock-capable 35-inch tires. Wrangler 4xe (PHEV) and Wrangler Moab 392 (V8) are split as separate models. Powertrains: 3.6L V6 (285 hp, standard on most), 2.0L turbo I4 (270 hp, standard on Sahara). 5,000-lb tow rating across the lineup.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts the 2026 Wrangler will be less reliable than the average new car, based on 2023-2025 owner data. Jeep brand finished near the bottom of the 2026 JD Power VDS at 267 PP100 (industry avg ~190 PP100); infotainment was the largest problem area.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 APEAL score specific to the Wrangler. Owner sentiment skews positive on off-road capability and styling, negative on ride comfort and tech reliability.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to call the Wrangler the segment benchmark for off-road capability â€” locking diffs, sway-bar disconnect, fording depth and trail breakovers are class-best â€” while flagging on-road compromises (wind noise, vague steering, rough ride, mediocre fuel economy) and a higher starting price than competitors. The refreshed 2026 cabin and 12.3-inch Uconnect 5 system are praised; technology-feature gaps and headlight performance below modern norms are common critiques.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owners average 3.0 stars across 33 2026-MY reviews. Praise focuses on off-road capability, the open-air experience, and the updated infotainment; complaints center on touchscreen glitches, ride quality, and dealer service.

notable_other: 12.3-inch Uconnect 5 touchscreen standard for 2026 (was 7-inch in early JL years)
Open-air freedom is the Wrangler's signature

trims.notes: Base trim (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 4-door pricing shown; 2-door Sport starts at $34,895+dest. EPA 49856 covers 4-door 3.6L V6 6MT (the standard powertrain mix on Sport). 8AT 4-door V6 is EPA 49855 (20 mpg combined). 2.0L turbo I4 is optional on Sport. NHTSA has not published a 2026 Wrangler overall rating. IIHS does NOT qualify Wrangler for TSP due to removable doors / fold-down windshield (IIHS frontal small-overlap good driver / acceptable passenger; moderate overlap good; side good; headlights marginal-to-poor).

notable_other: Adds 17-inch alloy wheels, remote keyless entry, power windows/locks, deep-tint rear glass vs Sport

trims.notes: Step-up trim. 2-door Sport S starts at $39,240+dest. Sport S keeps Command-Trac part-time 4WD.

notable_other: Adds 17-inch black steel wheels + 33-inch mud-terrain tires vs Sport S
Tru-Lok rear locking differential (Dana 44 rear axle)

trims.notes: Step-up trim, off-road-styled appearance package on Sport bones with real off-road hardware (locker + tires + rails). 2-door Willys starts at $42,715+dest. Willys '41 is an appearance sub-package on Willys per Stellantis press kit.

notable_other: Adaptive cruise + AEB + blind spot standard (first trim with ADAS suite)

trims.notes: Comfort/luxury-focused trim. First trim with adaptive cruise + AEB standard. Sahara is 4-door only â€” there is no 2-door Sahara in 2026. EPA 49854 covers Wrangler 4-door 2.0T 8AT.

notable_other: Rock-Trac NV241OR HD 4WD with 4:1 low-range transfer case
Tru-Lok electronic locking front + rear differentials
33-inch all-terrain tires standard, 35-inch optional via Xtreme 35 package
12.9-inch ground clearance with 35-inch tires; standard 10.8 inches

trims.notes: Off-road performance trim. Same 3.6L V6 as base. 2-door Rubicon starts at $45,970+dest. Optional Xtreme 35 package adds 35-inch tires and beadlock-capable wheels.

notable_other: Xtreme 35 package standard â€” 35-inch BFGoodrich KO2 tires + beadlock-capable wheels
Leather-trimmed heated seats + heated steering wheel standard

trims.notes: Top off-road luxury trim of the ICE Wrangler line. 2-door Rubicon X starts at $57,320+dest. Comes with same 3.6L V6 as Rubicon.

models.notes: Wrangler is offered in 2-door and 4-door body styles. This entry uses 4-door pricing as the primary (dominant configuration with all trims available); 2-door availability and pricing noted per trim. Sahara and Moab 392 are 4-door only. Wrangler 4xe (PHEV) and Wrangler Moab 392 (V8) are split as separate models per the multi-powertrain and performance-variant rules. Willys '41 is a sub-package on Willys per Stellantis press kit, not split as a separate trim. 85th Anniversary Edition is a limited-edition appearance package on Sport S 4-door (Twelve4Twelve program â€” one new limited edition each month for 2026), not a separate trim. Wrangler does not qualify for IIHS Top Safety Pick due to removable doors and fold-down windshield. NHTSA has not published a 2026 overall rating. Stellantis warranty: 3yr/36k basic, 5yr/60k powertrain. Jeep Wave program varies by trim; standard package not detailed in this build.

models.generation_context: JL-gen Wrangler PHEV (4xe) introduced for 2021 MY. 2026 MY is the final year of the 4xe program â€” Stellantis announced January 9, 2026 that it will phase out PHEV (4xe) programs in North America beginning with the 2026 MY in favor of hybrid and range-extended powertrains. Wrangler 4xe is sold alongside the gas Wrangler with its own jeep.com model page and unique trim hierarchy.
models.model_summary: Plug-in hybrid version of the JL Wrangler: a 2.0L turbocharged I-4 engine plus two electric motors and a 17.3-kWh lithium-ion battery deliver a combined 375 hp / 470 lb-ft. EPA-rated 21 miles all-electric range and 49 MPGe combined; charge-sustaining 20 MPG combined. Available only as a 4-door body. Five trims: Sport S 4xe / Willys 4xe / Sahara 4xe / Rubicon 4xe / Rubicon X 4xe. Selec-Trac full-time 4WD on Sport S/Sahara; Rock-Trac 4:1 transfer case on Rubicon/Rubicon X. J1772 AC charging only (~7.2 kW); no DC fast charging.

reliability.summary: Wrangler 4xe was the subject of a large 320,000+ unit recall covering battery fire risk, which was a factor in Stellantis' decision to discontinue the PHEV program after 2026 MY. Consumer Reports predicts 2026 Wrangler reliability will be below average; the PHEV variant adds high-voltage system complexity on top of base Wrangler issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 APEAL score for Wrangler 4xe specifically. Owner sentiment is mixed on the PHEV experience: praise for instant electric torque off-road and home-charge running cost; complaints on charge-port placement and software glitches.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers find the 4xe powertrain genuinely capable off-road â€” instant electric torque is well-suited to slow rock crawling â€” and the gas-electric system can deliver around 49 MPGe when charged daily and 20 MPG when run as a hybrid only. Critiques include the modest ~21-mile electric range relative to newer PHEVs, the 4xe pricing premium of roughly $10-15k over comparable gas Wrangler trims, and the lack of DC fast charging. The 2026 MY is the announced final year for the 4xe.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds 2026 sample is small and inherits the broader Wrangler complaint pattern. 4xe-specific praise is for electric off-road torque; criticism is for charge port location, infotainment touchscreen issues, and the cancellation announcement.

notable_other: 17.3 kWh battery; ~21 mi all-electric EPA range
Charge port located on driver-side front fender
J1772 AC charging only (~7.2 kW Level 2)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Wrangler 4xe PHEV powertrain line (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EPA 50264 covers all 4-door Wrangler 4xe trims (same powertrain across trim grades). Powertrain.horsepower_source set to manufacturer-combined per PHEV convention since 375 hp is the system combined output. PHEV: charge-sustaining 20 MPG combined goes in fuel_economy.*; charge-depleting 49 MPGe goes in ev_specifics.mpge_combined.

notable_other: Adds 17-inch black steel wheels + 33-inch mud-terrain tires
Willys '41 sub-package adds black appearance accents

trims.notes: Same 4xe powertrain as Sport S 4xe. Willys '41 4xe is a sub-package on Willys 4xe per Stellantis press kit.

notable_other: Adaptive cruise + AEB + blind spot standard
Heated front seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat

trims.notes: Comfort/luxury Wrangler 4xe trim. Same 4xe powertrain as Sport S 4xe.

notable_other: Rock-Trac HD 4WD with 4:1 low-range transfer case
Tru-Lok electronic locking front + rear differentials
33-inch all-terrain tires; 35-inch optional via Xtreme 35 package

trims.notes: Off-road performance 4xe trim. Same 4xe powertrain as Sport S 4xe.

notable_other: Xtreme 35 package standard â€” 35-inch BFGoodrich KO2 tires + beadlock-capable wheels
Leather-trimmed heated seats + heated steering wheel

trims.notes: Top off-road luxury 4xe trim. Same 4xe powertrain as Sport S 4xe.

models.notes: Wrangler 4xe is split as its own model from the gas Wrangler per the multi-powertrain rule (own jeep.com /wrangler/wrangler-4xe.html page). 4-door body only. The 4xe program is announced as the final 4xe MY (Stellantis press, Jan 9 2026); 2027 MY will move to ICE-only and range-extended replacement platforms. Per EPA, all 4xe trims share test ID 50264 (same powertrain across trim grades). Wrangler 4xe was the subject of multiple high-voltage battery recalls (320,000+ units) which factored into the cancellation. PHEV MPGe (49) goes in ev_specifics.mpge_combined; charge-sustaining 20 MPG goes in fuel_economy.* per project PHEV convention. J1772 AC charging only (~7.2 kW Level 2); no DC fast charging. Willys '41 4xe and High Altitude 4xe are sub-packages, not separate trims per the primary press release.

models.generation_context: New 2026 limited-edition V8 Wrangler â€” the successor to the discontinued 2025 Wrangler Rubicon 392. Has its own jeep.com /twelve4twelve/wrangler-moab-392 page. Treated as a separate model from the gas Wrangler per the performance-variant rule. 4-door only.
models.model_summary: Limited-edition V8 performance Wrangler. 6.4L 'Apache' HEMI V8 (470 hp / 470 lb-ft) paired with an 8-speed automatic and Selec-Trac full-time 2.72:1 transfer case. Standard 35-inch all-terrain tires on 17-inch beadlock-capable wheels. Body-color hardtop, rock rails, color-matched fender flares, two-mode exhaust, and a cold-air intake with hydro-guide water separator. Priced ~$20,000 below the outgoing Rubicon 392 thanks to a less specialized off-road hardware set than its predecessor. 4-door only.

reliability.summary: New trim with no MY-specific reliability data. JD Power 2026 VDS for the Jeep brand (267 PP100) is below average; the V8 Wrangler historically shows fewer high-voltage and infotainment issues than the 4xe but inherits the base JL platform's solid-axle truck noise/vibration character.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Wrangler Moab 392 or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Early press coverage frames the Moab 392 as a more accessibly-priced V8 Wrangler successor to the Rubicon 392 â€” it keeps the 470 hp Apache V8 character but trades the Rubicon's 4:1 transfer case for a 2.72:1 Selec-Trac full-time setup. Reviewers note the soundtrack and instant-throttle character are signature traits and that the cabin is upscale with leather seats, Alpine audio and the 12.3-inch Uconnect 5 screen. Critiques: EPA-rated 14 MPG combined on premium fuel; limited 2026 production allocation.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner reviews for the new Moab 392 trim at the time of research.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 315/70R17 all-terrain (35-inch) beadlock-capable, Low Gloss Smoked Bronze

notable_other: Cold-air intake with hydro-guide water separator (fording capable)
4.56:1 axle ratio, Selec-Trac full-time 2.72:1 transfer case
17-inch beadlock-capable wheels (Low Gloss Smoked Bronze) + 35-inch all-terrain tires
Heated Nappa leather seats; Alpine 9-speaker audio
Black tow hooks, color-matched fender flares, rock rails standard
Limited production via Jeep's Twelve4Twelve program

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 4-door body only. EPA 50127 confirmed: 6.4L V8 8AT 4WD, premium fuel, 14 MPG combined. Replaces the 2025 Wrangler Rubicon 392 at ~$20K lower starting price; uses Selec-Trac 2.72:1 full-time transfer case rather than Rubicon's 4:1 Rock-Trac. Production-limited via Jeep's 2026 Twelve4Twelve special-editions program. Performance variant per project rule â€” own model entry rather than Wrangler trim.

models.notes: Performance variant of Wrangler split as separate model per project rule. Sole trim. 4-door body only. V8 powertrain. Replaces the outgoing 2025 Rubicon 392 V8 Wrangler. Limited 2026 production via the Twelve4Twelve series. NHTSA has not published a 2026 overall rating; IIHS does not award TSP to Wrangler (removable doors/windshield).

models.generation_context: JT-gen Gladiator on sale since 2020. 2026 MY is a carryover year aligned with the Wrangler refresh â€” new quick-release door hinges, new exterior colors, and a new Willys '41 sub-package. The Gladiator shares its powertrain (3.6L Pentastar V6 with 8AT) and front-cab architecture with the Wrangler; the rear of the frame is unique to the Gladiator with a 5-foot bed.
models.model_summary: Mid-size body-on-frame pickup based on the Wrangler JL platform; 5-door crew cab with a 5-foot bed. Single 3.6L V6 powertrain (285 hp / 260 lb-ft, 8-speed automatic, 4WD standard) across all eight trims. Best-in-class 7,700 lb tow rating; 1,720 lb payload max. Trim ladder: Sport / Sport S / Willys / Sahara / Mojave / Rubicon / Mojave X / Rubicon X. Off-road variants: Rubicon (Rock-Trac) and Mojave (Desert Rated with FOX bypass shocks).

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability for the 2026 Gladiator. Jeep brand finished near the bottom of the 2026 JD Power VDS (267 PP100). Gladiator-specific concerns historically focus on death-wobble issues with solid front axles (multiple recalls 2020-2023) and infotainment.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Gladiator or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Gladiator's open-air pickup formula â€” removable doors and roof on a real off-road truck â€” and its segment-leading tow rating. Critiques include a long wheelbase that compromises some off-road geometry vs Wrangler, ride harshness, narrow 5-foot bed, and the absence of the discontinued 3.0L EcoDiesel option. Mojave's FOX dampers are the standout sub-segment feature for desert running.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews for 2026 MY are sparse early in the year. Carryover sentiment from 2024-2025 owners: praise for unique formula, off-road capability, and tow rating; complaints around ride harshness, fuel economy, and dealer-service consistency.

notable_other: 5-foot composite bed with bed-mounted utility rails

trims.notes: Sole-powertrain base trim (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EPA 49837 covers Gladiator 4WD 3.6L V6 8AT. Truck-bed schema fields populated per spec Â§3.8 (bed_length_in + bed_volume_cuft). NHTSA has not published a 2026 overall rating; IIHS does not award TSP to Gladiator (removable doors/windshield).

notable_other: Adds power windows + power door locks + remote keyless entry

trims.notes: Step-up trim. Adds creature comforts on Sport bones.

notable_other: Adds 17-inch black steel wheels + 33-inch mud-terrain tires

trims.notes: Off-road-appearance trim on Sport bones. Willys '41 is a sub-package per Stellantis press kit.

notable_other: Late 2026 trim addition â€” replaces previous Overland
Leather-trimmed heated seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat
Adaptive cruise + AEB + blind spot suite

trims.notes: New 2026 Gladiator trim. Comfort/luxury-focused replacement for the previous Overland tier.

notable_other: Reinforced front frame + cast-iron front control arms

trims.notes: Desert-focused off-road trim. Towing rating reduced versus Sport per Mojave suspension tuning.

notable_other: Rock-Trac HD 4WD with 4:1 low-range transfer case
Tru-Lok electronic locking front + rear differentials

trims.notes: Trail Rated off-road trim. Towing reduced to 7,000 lb (vs 7,700) per Rubicon-specific lower gearing/axles.

notable_other: Heated Nappa leather seats + heated steering wheel
Adaptive cruise + AEB + blind spot suite

trims.notes: Premium Mojave with luxury content.

notable_other: Leather-trimmed heated seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat

trims.notes: Premium Rubicon with Xtreme 35 package.

models.notes: 8 trims (Sport / Sport S / Willys / Sahara / Mojave / Rubicon / Mojave X / Rubicon X) all share the single 3.6L V6 / 8AT powertrain. Sahara is a 2026 trim re-addition replacing the previous Overland tier. Willys '41 is a sub-package on Willys, not split as a separate trim. Truck bed schema fields (bed_length_in, bed_volume_cuft) used per spec Â§3.8. NHTSA has not published a 2026 overall rating. IIHS does not award TSP to Gladiator (removable doors/windshield). Stellantis warranty: 3yr/36k basic, 5yr/60k powertrain.

models.generation_context: WL-gen Grand Cherokee on sale since 2022; 2026 MY introduces the new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo engine (324 hp / 332 lb-ft) as the standard powertrain on most trims, with the 3.6L Pentastar V6 (293 hp) retained only on the entry Laredo and Laredo X. Trailhawk and Overland 4xe (PHEV) variants are discontinued for 2026 â€” see separate Grand Cherokee 4xe model entry for the final-MY PHEV variant.
models.model_summary: Two-row mid-size SUV anchoring Jeep's volume luxury offering. 2026 MY adds the all-new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo (324 hp / 332 lb-ft, 21 city / 27 hwy / 23 combined) as standard on Laredo Altitude and above; 3.6L Pentastar V6 (293 hp, 19/26/22 mpg) continues on Laredo and Laredo X only. RWD standard, 4x4 available across the line. Trim ladder: Laredo / Laredo X / Laredo Altitude / Limited / Limited Reserve / Overland / Summit / Summit Reserve. Towing up to 6,200 lb with Hurricane 4.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability for the 2026 Grand Cherokee. The current WL platform has had multiple infotainment software recalls; the new Hurricane 4 turbo is a fresh powertrain in the WL with no established field reliability data.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Grand Cherokee or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers find the 2026 Grand Cherokee meaningfully improved over the 2022-2025 V6-only era: the new Hurricane 4 delivers stronger acceleration than the V6 and better mileage, though some critics note the turbo-four sounds and feels less refined than the V6 it replaces, particularly under heavy load. Cabin quality at Summit and Summit Reserve trims is benchmark-good; mid-grade Laredo trims show their price points. Towing is reduced vs the discontinued 5.7L V8 from 7,200 to 6,200 lb.

owner_reviews.summary: 2026-MY owner sample is sparse. Carryover 2024-2025 sentiment: praise for interior quality and capable AWD, complaints about Uconnect glitches and reliability.

notable_other: Forward Collision Warning Plus and active lane management standard
Blind-Spot Monitoring with Rear Cross Path

trims.notes: Base trim of the 2026 Grand Cherokee 2-row (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 3.6L Pentastar V6 is retained on Laredo and Laredo X only â€” all other trims use the new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo for 2026. EPA 50163 covers Grand Cherokee 4WD 3.6L V6. NHTSA 2024 5-star overall (most recent test); IIHS TSP earned for 2024 MY â€” assumed to carry to 2026 absent a 2026 IIHS retest (same generation/structure).

notable_other: Adds heated front seats + heated steering wheel

trims.notes: Mid-Laredo trim. Last 2026 GC trim that retains the 3.6L Pentastar V6 â€” Laredo Altitude and up move to 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo.

notable_other: All-new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo standard (324 hp / 332 lb-ft)
Hands-free Active Driving Assist (Level 2 ACC + lane keep)

trims.notes: First trim with the new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo and the 12.3-inch screen for 2026. EPA 50160 covers Grand Cherokee 4WD Hurricane 4 (23 mpg combined). Adds Level 2 ADAS.

notable_other: Adds memory seats with driver/passenger 12-way power

trims.notes: First Grand Cherokee trim with leather and ventilated front seats standard.
Upper Limited trim with Nappa leather and panoramic roof.

notable_other: Adds Selec-Terrain traction management (Auto / Sport / Snow / Rock / Mud-Sand)
Adds Nappa leather + ventilated front + 2nd row heated

trims.notes: Off-road oriented luxury trim with Quadra-Lift air suspension standard.

Quadra-Trac II with rear ELSD transfer case + low-range

trims.notes: Volume-luxury flagship trim with McIntosh audio.

notable_other: Exclusive Palermo Nappa leather color/material packages
Night Vision with pedestrian/animal detection standard

trims.notes: Top-luxury Grand Cherokee. Identical Hurricane 4 powertrain as Summit.

models.notes: 8 trims (Laredo / Laredo X / Laredo Altitude / Limited / Limited Reserve / Overland / Summit / Summit Reserve). 2-row Grand Cherokee uses the 116.7-inch wheelbase WL platform; the 3-row Grand Cherokee L (separate model) uses a stretched 121.7-inch wheelbase. 2026 MY introduces the 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo as standard on Laredo Altitude and above â€” only Laredo and Laredo X retain the 3.6L Pentastar V6. 4xe PHEV variant is split as a separate model (Grand Cherokee 4xe) per the multi-powertrain rule and is announced as the final 4xe MY. Trailhawk trim discontinued for 2026. NHTSA 5-star overall rating from 2024 carries forward; IIHS Top Safety Pick from 2024 likewise assumed to carry given the same generation/structure (no 2026 IIHS retest yet).

models.generation_context: Three-row WL-platform Grand Cherokee L on sale since 2021. 2026 MY adds the new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo as standard on Laredo Altitude and above, same as 2-row Grand Cherokee. Treated as a separate model from the 2-row GC because Jeep markets them as distinct lines with their own jeep.com pages.
models.model_summary: Three-row mid-size SUV; the long-wheelbase (121.7-inch) Grand Cherokee variant. Seven passenger seating with available 2nd-row captain chairs (6-pass) or bench (7-pass). Same powertrain ladder as 2-row: 3.6L Pentastar V6 (293 hp) on Laredo/Laredo X; new 2.0L Hurricane 4 Turbo (324 hp) on Altitude and above. Trim ladder: Laredo / Laredo X / Laredo Altitude / Limited / Limited Reserve / Overland / Summit / Summit Reserve. Towing 6,200 lb with Hurricane 4. There is no Grand Cherokee L 4xe â€” the PHEV is only offered in the 2-row configuration.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability for the 2026 Grand Cherokee L. Same WL-platform recall history as 2-row Grand Cherokee (infotainment software, occasional electrical issues).

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Grand Cherokee L or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Grand Cherokee L for combining genuine 7-passenger family utility with off-road credibility (Trail Rated Overland trim) and Jeep's upscale interior treatments at Summit/Summit Reserve levels. Critiques mirror the 2-row: limited 3rd row legroom vs Ford Explorer / Toyota Grand Highlander, the new Hurricane 4 turbo is unrefined under load, and Uconnect glitches persist.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient 2026-MY owner reviews. Carryover 2024-2025 sentiment is positive on interior + capability, negative on infotainment.

notable_other: Standard Level 1 ADAS suite (AEB, lane keep, blind spot)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Grand Cherokee L (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 7-seater with 60/40 bench 3rd row. EPA 50162 covers GC L 4WD V6. 2024 NHTSA 5-star + 2024 IIHS TSP carry forward in absence of 2026 retest.

notable_other: Adds heated front seats + heated steering wheel
Last GC L trim retaining the 3.6L V6

trims.notes: Mid-Laredo GC L trim. Last trim with 3.6L V6.

notable_other: Adds Level 2 Active Driving Assist (ACC + lane keep)

trims.notes: First trim with new Hurricane 4 Turbo. EPA 50159 covers GC L Hurricane 4 4WD.

notable_other: Adds memory seats + ventilated front seats

trims.notes: First GC L trim with standard leather and ventilated front seats.
Adds 2nd-row captain's chairs (reduces seating to 6) and Nappa leather.
Trail Rated 3-row trim with air suspension.

notable_other: Palermo Nappa leather + 24-way power massaging front seats

trims.notes: Volume-luxury 3-row flagship with McIntosh audio.
Top-luxury 3-row Grand Cherokee L.

models.notes: Three-row variant of Grand Cherokee on stretched 121.7-inch wheelbase. 8 trims (Laredo / Laredo X / Laredo Altitude / Limited / Limited Reserve / Overland / Summit / Summit Reserve) â€” same as 2-row. No 4xe PHEV variant is offered in the 3-row body. EPA 50159 (Hurricane 4 4WD, 22 mpg combined) and 50162 (V6 4WD, 21 mpg combined) verified individually. 2024 NHTSA 5-star + 2024 IIHS TSP carry forward.

models.generation_context: PHEV variant of the WL-platform Grand Cherokee, originally launched for 2022 MY. 2026 MY is the final year of the Grand Cherokee 4xe per Stellantis' January 2026 announcement to phase out PHEV programs in North America beginning with the 2026 MY. The 4xe retains the GME-T4 2.0L turbo I4 + dual-motor system (the new Hurricane 4 turbo is gas-only) and is offered only in the 2-row body. Trim lineup simplified for 2026 to Limited / Trailhawk / Summit (the prior Anniversary / Overland / Summit Reserve 4xe trims are gone).
models.model_summary: Plug-in hybrid 2-row Grand Cherokee â€” pairs a 2.0L turbo I4 (GME-T4) with two electric motors and a 17.3 kWh battery for a combined 375 hp / 470 lb-ft. EPA-rated 25 miles all-electric, 56 MPGe combined; charge-sustaining 23 MPG combined. 470-mile total range. Quadra-Trac standard. Trim ladder: Limited 4xe / Trailhawk 4xe / Summit 4xe. Trailhawk 4xe is the off-road-tuned 4xe with rear ELSD, Quadra-Lift air suspension, and Selec-Terrain. J1772 AC charging only (~7.2 kW); no DC fast charging.

reliability.summary: Same WL-platform recall history as gas Grand Cherokee plus shared 4xe high-voltage battery recall covering both Wrangler and Grand Cherokee 4xe â€” a factor in Stellantis' decision to discontinue PHEV.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers see Grand Cherokee 4xe as the segment's most off-road-capable PHEV (Trailhawk trim with Quadra-Lift and Selec-Terrain), with strong daily-driver electric range and combined fuel economy that beats the gas WL. 2026 MY is the final 4xe year and brings minor trim adjustments. Critiques: 4xe is ~$10-12K more expensive than equivalent gas Grand Cherokee trims, charge port awkward, no DC fast charging, and J1772 AC at 7.2 kW means a long charge time on Level 1.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews for 2026 MY are limited. Carryover 2024-2025 4xe sentiment: positive on combined electric+gas mileage and quiet city driving, negative on charge port and software issues.

notable_other: 17.3 kWh battery; 25 mi EPA all-electric range
Charge port on driver-side front fender
J1772 AC charging (~7.2 kW Level 2); no DC fast charging

trims.notes: Base trim of the Grand Cherokee 4xe PHEV powertrain line (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EPA 50265 covers Grand Cherokee 4xe. PHEV: charge-sustaining 23 MPG combined goes in fuel_economy.* and charge-depleting 56 MPGe goes in ev_specifics.mpge_combined. Inherits 2024 NHTSA 5-star + IIHS TSP from base Grand Cherokee body. powertrain.horsepower_source = manufacturer-combined per PHEV convention.
Off-road tuned 4xe trim with Trail Rated badge.

notable_other: Palermo Nappa leather + 24-way power massaging front seats

trims.notes: Top-luxury 4xe trim. Same 4xe powertrain as Limited 4xe.

models.notes: PHEV variant of Grand Cherokee split as separate model per multi-powertrain rule. 2026 MY is the final 4xe year (Stellantis press, Jan 9 2026). Trim lineup simplified for 2026 to Limited 4xe / Trailhawk 4xe / Summit 4xe â€” Anniversary 4xe / Overland 4xe / Summit Reserve 4xe trims discontinued. All trims share EPA test ID 50265 (same powertrain). Uses the older GME-T4 2.0L turbo I4 (not the new Hurricane 4 in gas Grand Cherokee). J1772 AC charging only; no DC fast charging. PHEV MPGe (56) in ev_specifics.mpge_combined; gas MPG (23) in fuel_economy.*. 2024 NHTSA 5-star + 2024 IIHS TSP inherited from base Grand Cherokee body.

models.generation_context: Current-gen MP-platform Compass on sale since 2017 (refreshed 2022). 2026 MY is the outgoing US generation â€” the all-new Stellantis STLA Medium next-gen Compass was revealed May 2025 but is not on US sale due to tariff uncertainty on its Canadian production (Brampton Assembly). 2026 US lineup drops the previous base Sport trim, shifts entry to Latitude, and adds Latitude Altitude and Limited Altitude blacked-out appearance trims.
models.model_summary: Compact SUV with a 2.0L turbocharged 4-cylinder (200 hp / 221 lb-ft) and 8-speed automatic, 4WD standard across all trims. Five trims for 2026: Latitude, Latitude Altitude, Limited, Limited Altitude, Trailhawk. The Trailhawk adds Active Drive Low 4WD with 20:1 crawl ratio for off-road duty. The next-gen Compass (built in Brampton, Canada) was revealed but is not on US sale for 2026 â€” current-gen carries over.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability. The current-gen Compass has had recurring transmission, electrical, and infotainment recall history. Jeep brand finished near the bottom of 2026 JD Power VDS (267 PP100).

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Compass or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers find the 2026 Compass adequate for the segment with standard 4WD as a differentiator, but a step behind newer compact-SUV rivals (Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4) on cabin quality, ride refinement, and infotainment polish. Trailhawk adds genuine off-road credibility unusual in the class. Critiques include the aging interior design, mediocre fuel economy for the class, and the discontinuation of the entry Sport trim that has pushed starting price north of $30K.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment is mixed: positive on standard 4WD, value vs price, and Trailhawk off-road capability; negative on infotainment glitches and ride quality.

notable_other: Standard AEB + adaptive cruise + lane keep + blind spot (Level 1 ADAS)
Standard heated front seats + remote start

trims.notes: Base trim of Compass (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). Previous Sport trim discontinued for 2026, making Latitude the new entry. EPA 49502 covers Compass 4WD 2.0T. 2024 IIHS TSP earned; NHTSA hasn't tested 2026 MY.

notable_other: Adds gloss-black exterior badges + 19-inch black wheels
Adds synthetic-leather seating + heated steering wheel + power driver seat

trims.notes: Blacked-out appearance package on Latitude bones.
Mid-tier luxury Compass.

notable_other: Limited content + Altitude blacked-out exterior package

trims.notes: New 2026 Compass trim combining Limited content with Altitude appearance.

notable_other: Trail Rated badge with Active Drive Low 4WD (20:1 crawl ratio)
Heated synthetic-leather seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat (now standard for 2026)

trims.notes: Off-road trim with Trail Rated badge. 2026 adds remote start + heated seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat as standard equipment (previously optional).

models.notes: 5 trims (Latitude / Latitude Altitude / Limited / Limited Altitude / Trailhawk). Sport trim discontinued for 2026, raising entry MSRP to $30,990. Single 2.0L turbo powertrain across all trims. 4WD standard. Outgoing-gen US Compass â€” next-gen revealed May 2025 but not on US sale yet (built in Brampton, Canada, tariff uncertainty). 2024 IIHS TSP earned; NHTSA 2026 not tested. EPA 49502 verified for the 2.0T 4WD.

models.generation_context: All-new 5th-gen Cherokee revealed August 21, 2025. The Cherokee nameplate returns to Jeep's US lineup after a three-year hiatus (the KL-gen Cherokee was discontinued in 2023). Built on Stellantis' STLA Large platform shared with Dodge Hornet/Hornet R/T architecture but tuned for mid-size SUV duty. Standard hybrid powertrain marks Jeep's first full-time AWD hybrid.
models.model_summary: Reintroduced mid-size SUV with a single standard hybrid powertrain: 1.6L turbocharged I4 + two electric motors driving an eCVT, system output 210 hp / 230 lb-ft, full-time AWD standard. Four trims: Cherokee, Laredo, Limited, Overland. EPA-rated 37 mpg combined (39 city / 35 hwy) â€” best-in-class fuel economy for a mid-size SUV. 500+ mile range. Launched late 2025 at dealers, Limited and Overland first, with entry Cherokee and Laredo arriving early 2026.

reliability.summary: All-new generation with no MY-specific reliability data yet. Hybrid powertrain shares technology with Stellantis' broader e-Hybrid family. Jeep brand 2026 JD Power VDS overall is below average (267 PP100).

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Cherokee or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: First-drive reviews are positive on the hybrid powertrain's smooth power delivery and best-in-class 37 mpg, the new interior with 12.3-inch screen and refreshed materials, and the standard ADAS suite. Critiques: the entry price stepped up substantially from the previous KL-gen Cherokee (starting near $37K vs ~$30K in 2023), the eCVT can drone under load, and towing is limited to 3,500 lb vs the 4,500 lb V6 KL.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner reviews â€” model launched late 2025 / early 2026.

notable_other: Active Driving Assist (Level 2 ACC + lane keep) standard
Standard full-time AWD with rear axle disconnect

trims.notes: Base trim of the all-new 5th-gen Cherokee (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). All trims share the same 1.6L turbo hybrid powertrain â€” full sole-powertrain line. EPA 50161 covers Cherokee AWD hybrid. NHTSA + IIHS have not yet rated the new generation. EV specifics block populated for the hybrid battery although standard hybrid (not plug-in) â€” electric_range_mi null.

notable_other: Adds heated front seats + heated steering wheel + power driver seat

trims.notes: Mid-Cherokee trim with comfort upgrades.

notable_other: Adds memory seats + ventilated front seats

trims.notes: First Cherokee trim with leather and ventilated front seats.
Top trim with off-road styling and luxury content.

models.notes: All-new 2026 Cherokee â€” Cherokee nameplate returns after 3-year hiatus. 4 trims (Cherokee / Laredo / Limited / Overland), all share same 1.6L turbo hybrid + dual-motor eCVT AWD powertrain. Limited and Overland reached dealers late 2025; Cherokee and Laredo arrived early 2026. EPA 50161 verified. NHTSA + IIHS have not yet rated the new gen. Standard hybrid (not PHEV) â€” EV-specifics minimally populated (battery_capacity_kwh ~1.5 kWh is the hybrid system battery; electric_range_mi null as standard hybrid).

models.generation_context: Mid-cycle refresh for the WS-platform Grand Wagoneer (on sale since 2022). 2026 MY brings simplified trim naming â€” Series I/II/III names retire in favor of clearer Grand Wagoneer / Limited Altitude / Summit Obsidian / Upland / Summit Reserve names plus appearance sub-packages. Base price drops over $20,000 to $64,740 vs the 2025 model. The standard Wagoneer (non-Grand) is discontinued for 2026.
models.model_summary: Premium full-size body-on-frame SUV; up to 7-passenger seating. Standard powertrain is the twin-turbo 3.0L Hurricane I6 (420 hp / 468 lb-ft, ZF 8HP75 8-speed automatic), RWD standard, 4WD available. Up to 10,000-lb tow rating with HD trailer tow + 3.92 axle. An optional REEV (range-extended EV) powertrain joins later in the MY with 647 hp combined, ~145 mi EV range, 92-kWh battery â€” late availability noted as separate option, not yet on sale. Trim ladder: Grand Wagoneer / Limited Altitude / Upland / Limited Reserve / Summit Obsidian / Summit Reserve.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability. Current Wagoneer/Grand Wagoneer platform had multiple early-year electrical and software recalls. 2026 refresh is largely a facelift on the same architecture.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Grand Wagoneer or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers acknowledge Stellantis' $20,000+ base-price cut for 2026 makes the Grand Wagoneer notably more competitive against Cadillac Escalade and Lincoln Navigator. The 3.0L Hurricane I6 (420 hp) replaces both the 5.7L V8 and previous 3.0L variants and offers strong performance with improved efficiency. Critiques: the platform's age is showing vs newest GM full-size SUVs; ride is firmer than Escalade; software/infotainment glitches persist. The REEV variant is highly anticipated but not yet at dealers.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment from prior MY mirrors the platform: praise for cabin appointments, ride and capability; complaints around tech reliability.

notable_other: Capri leather (Sea Salt/Black or Black) standard
Active Driving Assist (Level 2) + ACC + lane keep standard
10,000-lb tow rating with HD tow package + 3.92 axle

trims.notes: Base trim (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EPA 50157 covers Grand Wagoneer 4WD (premium fuel, 19 mpg combined). $62,145 base RWD + $2,595 destination = $64,740 widely cited starting price. 4WD adds $3,000. 2023 NHTSA 4-star overall; 2024 IIHS TSP carries forward.
Limited content + Altitude appearance package; 4WD only.

notable_other: Palermo Nappa leather + massaging front + 2nd-row seats
Adds Quadra-Drive II 4WD with rear ELSD + low-range

trims.notes: Top-luxury Grand Wagoneer trim with McIntosh MX1375 reference audio.

models.notes: 3 named trims at launch (Grand Wagoneer / Limited Altitude / Summit Obsidian); Upland, Limited Reserve and Summit Reserve appearance packages have late availability per Stellantis press materials. Twin-turbo 3.0L Hurricane I6 standard across all trims; REEV (range-extended EV) variant is late-MY option not yet on sale, will produce 647 hp / 145 mi EV range / 92 kWh battery. The standard Wagoneer (non-Grand) is discontinued for 2026 â€” only Grand Wagoneer carries the nameplate. Premium fuel required. 2023 NHTSA 4-star + 2024 IIHS TSP carry forward.

models.generation_context: Long-wheelbase variant of the Grand Wagoneer with a stretched 130.0-inch wheelbase (vs 123.0 inches on standard). Same 2026 MY refresh as the standard Grand Wagoneer â€” simplified trim naming, +$3,000 per trim over standard wheelbase. Marketed as its own model line by Jeep with a dedicated jeep.com page.
models.model_summary: Long-wheelbase 3-row full-size luxury SUV. +12 inches in length and +7 inches in wheelbase over standard Grand Wagoneer for substantially expanded 3rd-row legroom and cargo volume. Same twin-turbo 3.0L Hurricane I6 (420 hp / 468 lb-ft) standard powertrain and trim ladder. Towing rating up to 9,800 lb.

reliability.summary: Same WS platform as standard Grand Wagoneer â€” see same reliability profile.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Grand Wagoneer L or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Grand Wagoneer L as the most spacious full-size luxury SUV under $100K for the base trim, with substantial cargo + 3rd-row gain over standard wheelbase. Same powertrain and refresh story as standard.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient 2026 owner reviews. Prior-MY: positive on cabin and 3rd-row legroom; negative on tech.

notable_other: +12 inches in length vs standard Grand Wagoneer
Capri leather + Uconnect 5C NAV 12-inch + 12.3-inch driver display
9,800-lb tow rating with HD tow package

trims.notes: Base trim of Grand Wagoneer L (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). +$3,000 over standard wheelbase Grand Wagoneer. EPA 50158 covers Grand Wagoneer L 4WD (18 mpg combined, premium fuel). 2024 IIHS TSP shared with standard Grand Wagoneer.
Limited content + Altitude appearance on long-wheelbase.

notable_other: Palermo Nappa leather + massaging front + 2nd-row seats
Dark Waxed Walnut wood trim + open-pore finishes
Quadra-Drive II 4WD with rear ELSD + low-range

trims.notes: Top-luxury Grand Wagoneer L.

models.notes: Long-wheelbase variant of Grand Wagoneer with same powertrain and trim ladder (named with 'L' suffix here). Same 2026 refresh as standard. +$3,000 per trim over standard wheelbase. Upland L, Limited Reserve L, and Summit Reserve L sub-trims have late availability per Stellantis press materials. EPA 50158 verified for 4WD V6. 2024 IIHS TSP carried.

models.generation_context: All-new 2026 model. The Jeep Recon is Jeep's first dedicated Trail Rated battery-electric SUV on Stellantis' STLA Large platform shared with Wagoneer S. Revealed publicly in late 2025; production starts at Toluca (Mexico) Assembly in early 2026, US dealer arrivals planned summer/fall 2026. Launches in a single Moab trim at $65,000 base; additional trims with longer 250-mi range will follow later in 2026.
models.model_summary: All-electric mid-size SUV with the only Trail Rated EV badge in the market. 100.5 kWh battery (400V architecture), dual-motor AWD producing 650 hp / 620 lb-ft. 230 mi EPA estimated range on the launch Moab trim. 80 MPGe combined. CCS1 charging port (no native NACS); DC fast charging 5-80% in ~28 min. Removable doors, available Sky One-Touch Power Top, rear-quarter glass and swing-gate glass. Sole trim at launch (Moab); longer-range variants coming later in 2026.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS for the Jeep brand: 267 PP100 (industry avg 204). 25th of 26 mainstream/luxury brands ranked (4th from bottom). Industry avg 204 PP100. Ranked 24th of 25 in CR 2026 brand rankings - among the lowest in reliability and owner satisfaction. Dead last on owner satisfaction for 3rd year in a row. No 2026-MY model-level data is published for this specific model; assessment is based on brand-level signals.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Jeep Recon or for the Jeep brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Pre-launch coverage emphasizes the Recon as the first dedicated electric off-road SUV with a Trail Rated badge, slotting between the Wrangler and Wagoneer S. Critics flag the modest 230-mi launch range (250 mi planned for later trims) as below class leaders and the absence of native NACS as a Stellantis-wide gap on its EV strategy.

owner_reviews.summary: No owner reviews â€” production starts early 2026 and US dealer arrivals are scheduled summer/fall 2026.

notable_other: Trail Rated badge â€” only EV SUV with Trail Rated certification
Dual-motor AWD with electronic locking rear differential
Removable rear-quarter glass and swing-gate glass
Comfort Package included on Moab launch trim
CCS1 charging port; no native NACS (NACS-to-CCS1 adapter required for Supercharger)
Selec-Terrain traction modes including Rock and Sand-Snow

trims.notes: Sole launch trim (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EV: MPGe combined (80) mirrors into both fuel_economy.combined_mpg AND ev_specifics.mpge_combined per project EV convention. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 Jeep Recon entry as of 2026-05-13; sources.fuel_economy set to the brand model-browse page per spec Â§4 EPA-unavailable fallback. City/highway MPGe not yet broken out by EPA; only combined 80 MPGe published. Range estimate is manufacturer-claimed pending EPA verification. NHTSA + IIHS have not tested the Recon. powertrain.engine_config set to 'none' per EV convention; horsepower_source 'manufacturer-combined' per dual-motor EV convention. Production starts at Toluca early 2026 with US dealer arrivals planned summer/fall 2026 â€” Moab is currently the only available trim with longer-range variants planned later in 2026.

models.notes: All-new 2026 EV. Single launch trim (Moab) at $65,000. Additional trims with 250-mile range planned for later in 2026. CCS1 plug â€” no native NACS port. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 Recon entry â€” sources.fuel_economy uses the brand model-browse page per spec Â§4 EPA-unavailable fallback rule. NHTSA + IIHS have not tested. Trail Rated badge is unique among EV SUVs.
